description: MIF org GitHub-App auth manifest — single source of truth. Five least-privilege GitHub Apps mint short-lived installation tokens for org workflows. Each App is identified by its OAuth client-id (GitHub's current best practice; the legacy numeric app-id is deprecated and warns). Per app: id_variable = org Actions VARIABLE holding the OAuth client-id (Iv23...; public, so optional-App jobs may gate on it in if:); key_secret = org Actions SECRET holding the App private key (PEM); permissions = installed least-privilege set (Repository perms unless prefixed org:; metadata:read implicit); install_on = repos the App is installed on ('all' = every org repo, org-wide; the apps are installed org-wide and wired per gate as repos adopt the standard suite); consumers = workflows that mint this App's token. Tokens are minted with the SHA-pinned mint_action via its client-id input. Governed by ADR-011 (supersedes ADR-008); validated in CI by .github/workflows/app-manifest-validate.yml via inline jq cross-checks (SHA pin, role/name match, credential uniqueness, required fields, permission enums, no unknown keys, install_on, consumer paths). auth/apps.schema.json documents the manifest shape but is not executed in CI.

ci.purpose: General CI cross-repo identity. Reads org/sibling state the default GITHUB_TOKEN cannot — primary consumer is OpenSSF Scorecard reading branch protection (administration:read) so posture scores from real settings.

catalog.purpose: Marketplace catalog updates — re-pins external plugin entries to their latest attested release and opens auto-merge PRs against the catalog.

pages.purpose: Cross-repo org Pages deploy/notify — docs-source repos build then notify the github.io assembly repo to compose and deploy.

automerge.purpose: Dependabot auto-approve and merge. Dependabot cannot approve its own PR; this App supplies the non-Dependabot approval identity.

release.purpose: Authenticates the gh release / contents-write steps in release.yml. The keyless OIDC attestation (id-token + attestations) is unchanged — the attestation signer SAN stays the workflow, so gh attestation verify is unaffected. This App only authenticates the publish/contents steps.
